databaseQueries.query: SELECT `permissions`.*, `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` as `pivot_permission_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `permissions` inner join `model_has_permissions` on `permissions`.`id` = `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` WHERE `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` in (4) and `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'

SELECT `roles`.*, `model_has_roles`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_roles`.`role_id` as `pivot_role_id`, `model_has_roles`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `roles` inner join `model_has_roles` on `roles`.`id` = `model_has_roles`.`role_id` WHERE `model_has_roles`.`model_id` in (4) and `model_has_roles`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'
SELECT * FROM `agricultural_professionals` WHERE `agricultural_professionals`.`user_id` = 4 and `agricultural_professionals`.`user_id` IS not NULL LIMIT 1
SELECT * FROM `soil_analysis` ORDER BY `id` DESC LIMIT 10 offset 0
SELECT * FROM `soil_analysis_expert_comments` WHERE `soil_analysis_expert_comments`.`soil_analysis_id` = 4 and `soil_analysis_expert_comments`.`soil_analysis_id` IS not NULL ORDER BY `created_at` ASC

*attributes.recommendation:
PRIORITY 1 – CORRECT SOIL pH (Very Low)
Apply agricultural lime (CaCO₃) at 2–4 t/ha. Use dolomitic lime if Mg is also low.
Wait 3–6 weeks before applying fertilizers. Re-test soil after 3–6 months.

FERTILIZER APPLICATION SCHEDULE:
• Nitrogen (Low): Apply 60–90 kg N/ha in 2–3 split doses during the growing season.
• Phosphorus (Medium): Maintenance application 20–40 kg P₂O₅/ha. Monitor every 2–3 years.
• Potassium (Medium): Maintenance 20–40 kg K₂O/ha. Adequate for most crops.
• Organic Matter (Moderate): Continue regular compost additions (2–3 t/ha). Maintain residue management.

IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Correct soil pH FIRST before applying fertilizers – nutrient availability is maximized at pH 5.5–7.0.
• Split N and K applications into 2–3 doses during the growing season (especially on sandy soils).
• Building organic matter improves CEC, water retention, nutrient release, and pH buffering simultaneously.
• Re-test soil after 6–12 months following significant amendments (liming, heavy fertilization).

Source: BSWM-DA Philippines Soil Test Interpretation Guidelines; Horneck et al. (2011) OSU Extension; Landon (1991).

FERTILIZER APPLICATION SCHEDULE:
• Nitrogen (Low): Apply 60–90 kg N/ha in 2–3 split doses during the growing season.
• Phosphorus (Medium): Maintenance application 20–40 kg P₂O₅/ha. Monitor every 2–3 years.
• Potassium (Medium): Maintenance 20–40 kg K₂O/ha. Adequate for most crops.
• Organic Matter (Moderate): Continue regular compost additions (2–3 t/ha). Maintain residue management.

IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Correct soil pH FIRST before applying fertilizers – nutrient availability is maximized at pH 5.5–7.0.
• Split N and K applications into 2–3 doses during the growing season (especially on sandy soils).
• Building organic matter improves CEC, water retention, nutrient release, and pH buffering simultaneously.
• Re-test soil after 6–12 months following significant amendments (liming, heavy fertilization).

Source: BSWM-DA Philippines Soil Test Interpretation Guidelines; Horneck et al. (2011) OSU Extension; Landon (1991).

• Phosphorus (Medium): Maintenance application 20–40 kg P₂O₅/ha. Monitor every 2–3 years.
• Potassium (Medium): Maintenance 20–40 kg K₂O/ha. Adequate for most crops.
• Organic Matter (Moderate): Continue regular compost additions (2–3 t/ha). Maintain residue management.

IMPORTANT NOTES:
• Correct soil pH FIRST before applying fertilizers – nutrient availability is maximized at pH 5.5–7.0.
• Split N and K applications into 2–3 doses during the growing season (especially on sandy soils).
• Building organic matter improves CEC, water retention, nutrient release, and pH buffering simultaneously.
• Re-test soil after 6–12 months following significant amendments (liming, heavy fertilization).

Source: BSWM-DA Philippines Soil Test Interpretation Guidelines; Horneck et al. (2011) OSU Extension; Landon (1991).
